graphEvents.label: duplicated setEnv/restoreEnv helpers across test files

title: Missing JSDoc on exported getSnapshot function
scopeProof: getSnapshot is exported and consumed by memory-crud-health.ts via `import { getSnapshot as getRoutingTelemetrySnapshot }`. No JSDoc block exists. Contrast with recordInvocation (line 25) which has a JSDoc block.
recommendation: Add JSDoc describing the returned RoutingTelemetrySnapshot shape and that it is pure in-memory with no side effects.

title: Missing JSDoc on exported shouldPreserveBm25 function
scopeProof: shouldPreserveBm25 is exported (line 394) and tested via routeQuery integration. It calls classifyIntent and resolveArtifactClass internally. No JSDoc block exists. Contrast with shouldPreserveGraph (line 171-182) which has a comprehensive JSDoc block.
recommendation: Add JSDoc documenting that it checks intent (find_spec/find_decision) and resolves artifact class against BM25_PRESERVING_ARTIFACTS set.

title: Missing JSDoc on exported isGraphChannelPreservationEnabled
scopeProof: Function is exported (line 393) and tested directly (012-T2.6, 012-T2.7). Has inline comments but no formal JSDoc. The inline comment references REQ-008 which is useful but not machine-visible.
recommendation: Convert the inline comments at lines 155-159 into a JSDoc block with @returns boolean.

title: Module header stale — omits channel preservation overrides
scopeProof: The module header (lines 1-6) reads 'Tier-to-channel-subset routing for query complexity' and 'Maps classifier tiers to channel subsets for selective pipeline execution.' It does not mention the BM25 preservation override (line 144, shouldPreserveBm25) or the graph-channel preservation override (line 183, shouldPreserveGraph) that are the primary additions of this packet. A maintainer skimming the header would miss the preservation override layer.
recommendation: Append to module header: 'Also applies BM25 preservation for spec/artifact intent queries and graph-channel preservation for entity-dense queries.'

title: parseTriggerPhrases has asymmetric and undocumented fallback behavior
scopeProof: In entity-density.ts parseTriggerPhrases (lines 46-58): when JSON.parse(raw) throws → returns [raw] (line 55); when JSON.parse(raw) succeeds but returns non-array (object, number, string) → returns [] (line 57). These two fallback paths produce different results for semantically similar inputs. The catch path returns the raw string as a single-element array while the non-array parse path silently drops the value. Neither path is documented.
recommendation: Document the behavior asymmetry or unify to a single fallback strategy (e.g., always return [] on any parse anomaly).

title: Duplicate env-flag constant in same test file
scopeProof: `const FEATURE_FLAG = 'SPECKIT_COMPLEXITY_ROUTER'` at line 33 and `const COMPLEXITY_FLAG = 'SPECKIT_COMPLEXITY_ROUTER'` at line 415 both define the same env var string with different names. FEATURE_FLAG is used by pre-012 test blocks (T026-04 through T026-06); COMPLEXITY_FLAG is used by packet-012 test blocks (012-T1 through 006-T1). A maintainer running 'grep SPECKIT_COMPLEXITY_ROUTER' would find two bindings and have to resolve which is active.
recommendation: Consolidate to a single constant (either rename FEATURE_FLAG→COMPLEXITY_FLAG at line 33 or drop line 415 and reuse FEATURE_FLAG).

title: Duplicated setEnv/restoreEnv helpers across test files
scopeProof: Both test files define identical `function setEnv(key, value)` and `function restoreEnv()` helpers with the same `savedEnv` object pattern. The same pattern also exists in adaptive-fusion.vitest.ts:31-43 and learned-combiner.vitest.ts:34-46. A change to the env-save/restore contract would require editing multiple files.
recommendation: Extract setEnv/restoreEnv into a shared test utility (e.g., tests/helpers/env-test-utils.ts) and import from all test files.
